context: Tests whether the agent produces a well-formed conventional commit message with correct type selection (feat/fix over refactor), proper subject line formatting, and a body that explains why rather than restating what the diff shows.

checklist.name: Correct type priority
checklist.description: Uses 'fix' (not 'refactor') as the type, since the primary change is a bug fix even though some helper code was also restructured

checklist.name: Imperative subject verb
checklist.description: The subject line verb is imperative present tense (e.g. 'extend', 'fix', 'align') — NOT past tense ('fixed', 'extended') or third-person ('fixes', 'extends')

checklist.name: Subject length
checklist.description: Subject line (after the type prefix) is 72 characters or fewer in total

checklist.name: No trailing period
checklist.description: Subject line does NOT end with a period

checklist.name: Lowercase subject
checklist.description: The first word of the subject description (after the colon and space) is lowercase, unless it is a proper noun

checklist.name: No file paths in subject
checklist.description: The subject line does NOT contain a file path (e.g. 'src/auth/jwt.js' or similar)

checklist.name: Body explains why
checklist.description: The body contains an explanation of why the change was needed (e.g. the mismatch between JWT TTL and session TTL causing user logouts), not just a description of what the code now does

checklist.name: Body does not restate diff
checklist.description: The body does NOT simply list code-level changes that are already visible in the diff (e.g. does not say 'changed JWT_TTL to SESSION_TTL' or 'removed try/catch')

checklist.name: Body line wrap
checklist.description: Every line in the body is 72 characters or fewer

checklist.name: Blank line separation
checklist.description: There is a blank line between the subject and the body (if a body is present)

checklist.name: No vague subject
checklist.description: The subject does NOT contain any of the banned phrases: 'misc fixes', 'updates', 'changes', 'wip'
